arts.ttl: Cesc Fabregas: ‘We’re too nice, we need to commit more fouls’
arts.sum: Following the Lombardy Derby between Como and Monza, which ended in a 1-1 draw, Como’s head coach Cesc Fabregas shared his thoughts on the match and the team’s performance. The game, part of the 14th round of Serie A, showcased the struggles both teams face as they fight to avoid relegation. Fabregas began by discussing the challenges his team encountered during the match. “Today we didn’t have much depth on the bench. We are in a bit of a difficult moment with injuries,” he noted. arts.sum: And that has seen Ferguson remind players and fans not to get too carried away with their win on the Cote d'Azur - stating that Philippe Clement will have to come up with solutions to break down teams who sit behind the ball, as opposed to Nice who came and had a go at Rangers . arts.ttl: Rangers hit out at 'draconian' Nice security measures as furious statement damns treatment of travelling fans
arts.sum: With a security operation labelled as 'draconian', Rangers fans had power banks taken off them by 'heavy-handed' officers - and now Rangers have stated that they will remain in dialogue with UEFA over their next steps despite their strong win. A club statement read on Friday evening: "Rangers FC is deeply concerned over several unacceptable incidents in Nice last night where our supporters’ safety was jeopardised.
